Introduction
Welcome to Module 1.
!
Module 1 is all about the core strategies and tactics of Product Launch Formula.
!
If you don’t go through anything else, if you just go through this, what it’s going to
teach you is how to build a relationship with your prospects and your market... with
your clients... with your would-be clients... and really build a business by building that
relationship and conversation with them.
!
Everything in business is so much easier if you talk to your people and find out what
they want, work with them, help them out, and you will find out what they need next
and then you create that for them.
!
It all ties into your marketing, and your marketing ties into your product.
!
It will literally change the rest of your life if you truly absorb nothing else but what’s in
this module.
!
This core work is going to end up going into all of your launches and all your types of
launches.
!
No matter where you are right now - whether you don’t have a business whatsoever,
with no list and no product... whether you have a great business already with traffic and
a list... whether you’re a practitioner and you want to start leveraging your business...
whether you’re an artist - it doesn’t matter where you’re at, these core strategies and
tactics that we’re going to go through here are going to set you up to build that
relationship, to figure out exactly what people want, and give it to them both in your
marketing and in your product.
!
I know that’s a big promise, but that’s what this module is all about. It’s about creating
the core pieces that are then going to drop into your various launches as we move
through the later modules.
!
In the later modules we’ll be going through each different type of launch (Quick
Launches, Seed Launches, Internal Launches, JV Launches). The core work you do here
will move forward and go into all those other modules.
!
Now, we do have a lot of people that upgrade through each version of Product Launch
Formula. If you’re brand new, trust me, there are people watching this video who have
been with me since 2005 and have upgraded every step of the way.
!
If you’re upgrading, then what we’re covering here in Module 1 is going to be a review
and I’ve gotten a lot better at teaching this and gotten a lot more succinct. It’s a lot
sharper and there is new stuff. You definitely want to go through it.

"Crazy Like a Fox"
When I first started in business I started publishing about the stock market. That was
something that I had some expertise in. I had spent a lot of time studying the stock
market, but I didn’t have any credentials. I didn’t have any letters after my name. I
wasn’t a stock broker. I wasn’t a registered investment advisor. I didn’t have any
degrees in the stock market or anything. It was just something I had studied a lot.
!
I was desperate to start a business. I had learned about this whole idea of selling
information online, so I wanted to try it. It was something I knew about, so I started
publishing a newsletter.
!
Truth be told, I didn’t really have the wherewithal to sell something. I didn’t know how
to sell stuff. I didn’t have the self-confidence to start selling something, since I didn’t
have any letters after my name or anything like that.
!
So I just started publishing a newsletter. The first newsletter I sent out to all my friends
and family who had an email address. There were 19 email addresses for that first
newsletter, and actually one of those was my second email address and one was my
wife’s email address. Outside of my immediate family, 17 people got that newsletter.
!
I gradually built that up from there and it grew through word of mouth. I kept on
publishing this newsletter... and eventually I came out with paid versions of the
newsletter... but I always kept that free version of the newsletter.
!
At that time, I had this friend who had been in the business of publishing stock market
newsletters for a long time. In fact, his dad was in the business and that’s how he came
into the business. I remember him telling me that he couldn’t figure out why I was
publishing this free newsletter, because it was the best quality newsletter I could put
out at the time. It became pretty darn popular and actually grew through word of
mouth.
!

-- 5 of 46 --

He just thought it was insane that I was publishing this newsletter for free. He couldn’t
figure it out. He was charging $1,000 per month for his newsletter. He was like, “Why
would you do this?” He had this long history of publishing before the internet. It went
way back, and his dad’s newsletter went way back, and he couldn't figure it out.
!
A couple years later he said, “I used to think you were crazy, but it was sort of crazy
like a fox.” He could see I had built up this big platform and I had built up this big list.
!
In fact, he came around asking to partner with me to try to get my expertise. It was
just because of the strategy I had developed, and maybe it was because my business
grew up in the online era of giving away good information that eventually led into a
sale.
!
That one strategy has been a big win for me. It also taught me the real power of
strategy in your business. Sun Tzu, in the The Art of War, wrote “The battle is always
won before you set foot on the battlefield.” That’s really Product Launch Formula in one
sentence.
! “The battle is always won before you set foot on the battlefield.”
!
In other words, strategy is what wins it for you.
!
In Product Launch Formula, we give away great content and we build up trust. We build
up rapport and a relationship with the people that are getting our materials, that are on
our lists in the launch, and it sets up that sale. It’s a lot easier to sell when someone
trusts you. The launch is all about everything that leads up to the sale. That’s the battle
that’s won before you set foot on the battlefield. Before someone gets to your sales
letter, you’ve already won the battle, because they already trust you. That’s really what
Product Launch Formula is all about.
!
That’s what I did when I started publishing that newsletter. Even though I was giving
away this free newsletter, I was setting up the eventual sale. I put in the effort ahead of
time. I used that strategy of thinking further out than just trying to get the money right
now.
!
Product Launch Formula is about setting up that sale, but it’s also bigger.
!
It’s about making decisions for the long term. That’s the mindset I want you to get in as
we start Module 1. It’s not about making the money right now, it’s not about sending
that email and instantly how much money we’re going to make right now. It’s about
setting yourself up in a business that’s going to last.
!
This one thing will always set you apart, because so many other people are making the
money today. Their long term is making the money this week. If you just think a little
bit longer term and use your prelaunch to build that trust and rapport... use each
launch to build onto the next... then you are going to win.

-- 6 of 46 --

!
Product Launch Formula is great, because the PLF tactics can give you a quick cash
infusion. I’ve done this over and over. I’ve used it to finish my basement. I’ve used it as
a down payment on a new house. I’ve used it to pay my tax bill. Long term it’s the
strategy of sharing great content and creating trust before you ask for the sale... the
strategy of getting great results for your clients and customers... creating great
products based on that feedback that you’re getting from the market through your
launch conversation. It’s about putting together great promotions that cause great
leverage for you that bring in a lot of money. But also it could bring in a lot of money
for your partners.
!
Those strategies all create huge leverage going forward and they continually win. That
leverage builds on top of each other. It’s this big snowball rolling downhill, where your
launches get bigger and bigger.
!
You need to think about, “How will this action set up my next action?” In other words,
“What’s the purpose of this email?”
!
It may seem like it’s free to send that email to your list, but there’s always some cost
involved. Some people are going to unsubscribe... or if they look at your email and it’s
not impactful or meaningful to them, then maybe they won’t open your next email. So
there’s always some cost involved. What are you going to get out of that email? What’s
the purpose of that email, that blog post, that video? What’s the purpose of that
product? What is it going to get you now and in the future?
!
I always like to think about things on multiple time frames. “I’m sending this email,
what’s it going to do for me now? A week from now? A month from now? A year from
now?” I’m always thinking that. When I’m in a launch I’m thinking about, “What’s this
launch going to do for my next launch? How is it going to change my relationship with
the market? With my prospects? With the people who buy from me and are my new
customers? With my partners? How is it going to change my positioning? How is it
going to set up my next launch and the launch after that?”
!
As you’re looking at a launch - as you’re considering a product... a promotion... or some
angle to take - don’t just think about how much money you’re going to make. Think
about what your current clients are going to think about that. What your current
prospects are going to think about that. Think about the people that don’t buy from you
and what they’re going to think. Think about your joint venture partners and what
they’ll think. The people in your market just observing the whole market - observing
who is the expert... who is the guru... who has the positioning - what are they going to
think.
!
I’ve seen people screw this up over and over. A classic thing is someone will sell a
product, they’ll sell a bunch of that product, and then three months later they’ll give it
away for free or they’ll make it a bonus for some other offer. They’ll promote someone
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 7

-- 7 of 46 --

else and they’ll give it away for free. They’ll do one of those dollar offers where you can
get the product for a dollar, “pay $1.00 and we’ll put you into the membership site.”
!
How do all those people that bought your product a month ago that paid a lot of money
for that product feel when you give that product away for free?
!
You have to think more long term.
!
You don’t always want to be thinking about a year from now, because you do have to
bring in the money now. You have to build the sales now. You have to take care of your
partners now. You have to take care of your clients now. But how is it going to set you
up?
!
I love to get outdoors. I love to ski, I love to mountain bike, I love to white water
kayak. In almost any one of those activities, almost any kind of sport, the further you
can extend your horizon, you win. You just absolutely win and you will do better.
!
What I mean by extend your horizon is that if you’re skiing... if you’re going down the
slopes... you don’t want to be looking three feet in front of your skis. If you’re on a
mountain bike, you don’t want to be looking three feet in front of your front wheel. In a
kayak, you don’t want to be looking just in front of your bow. You want to look further
out. You want to be looking not at the next move, but two or three turns down the
path, down the river, down the slope.
That’s what the true experts do. They’re not reacting to whatever rock is in front of
them, they’re reacting to what’s coming in two or three turns down the path. That’s
how you judge an expert, that’s what the experts do. That’s how you become an
expert, by extending your horizon.
!
It’s the same way in your business. You don’t want to look at just, “What am I going to
be sending to my people tomorrow?” Yes, you have to get that done, you have to know
what you’re doing. But you want to extend your horizon. How is this email going to set
up the email next week? How’s that going to set up the video next month? How is it
going to set up the product two or three products from now?
!
You don’t want to obsess about this, but the further you extend your horizon the better
you are. The battle is won before you get on the battlefield. That was true when Sun
Tzu said it thousands of years ago, and it’s still true. Strategy is what wins in the long
run.
As we go through this module, we’re going to dig deep into the core PLF strategies and

that’s going to set up all of your launches.
!
In the next video, I have the number one cornerstone strategy that makes all of

Sideways Sales Letter
Now we get to the absolute core strategy inside Product Launch Formula.
!
It's what I call the Sideways Sales Letter.
!
If you’re brand new to Product Launch Formula you might be wondering, “What does
that mean? That sounds pretty weird.”
!
There are a lot of different paths through these strategies and tactics, but the one
common theme, the one common path for the most successful PLF owners, for the
people who really take this and put it to use, is the Sideways Sales Letter. That’s the
core strategy.
!
It’s the opposite of the old style sales letter.
!
Most of us have been on an old school sales letter. None of us ever go to a sales letter
intentionally, we end up on a sales letter. The web page will load, and then when it
loads, there's this scroll bar off to the side.
!
The scroll bar is really big when the page first starts loading, or if you have a slow
internet connection like I do. But then that sales letter starts loading up... and it loads
10 pages or 20 pages, maybe 30 or 40 pages... and that little scroll bar gets really small
as all those pages load, and you end up with this huge 30 page sales letter. They're
really long.
!
The thing is, people don’t tend to read those long sales letters. What they do is they
grab that scroll bar and they scroll down to the bottom to check what the price is and
then they decide whether they want to scroll back up and maybe read your headline.
Maybe they’ll read a few bullet points, and they’ll come back down to the offer. That’s
what generally happens in the long form sales letter, the old school sales letter.
!
There is a time and a place for a long form sales letter. I’m not saying it’s evil, or you
can’t do it, or that it absolutely doesn’t work. There is a time and a place for it. This is
sort of the old school marketing.
!
The Sideways Sales Letter is where we take this long vertical sales letter - maybe it’s 10
pages... 20 pages... or 30 pages - and we turn it sideways and we make it a 10 day
sequence. That’s the core of Product Launch Formula.
!
It's different from one sales message, when you send an email and you send people to
your sales page, your sales video or your sales letter, and you hope that they buy.
That’s what I call hope marketing. This is the opposite of hope marketing.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 9
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 9 of 46 --

Hope is a fantastic word. If you’re shipwrecked and you’re just drifting out on the sea
and you’re hoping someone comes to rescue you. In that kind of situation, hope can
literally keep you alive. But in business, you don’t want to depend on hope. You want to
engineer your success.
!
When you just email and send someone to a one off sales letter or a sales video, and
you’re hoping they buy, that doesn’t work. This is the Sideways Sales Letter, the new
style that converts now.
!
This is my depiction of a sideways letter. These three boxes are your prelaunch,
what we call our PLC – prelaunch content. This is a classic internal kind of launch, but
conceptually this will work for all your launches. What you’re doing is you’re putting out
content in each one of those.
!
This is your first piece of PLC, your second piece of PLC, and your third piece of PLC.
!
Typically in your prelaunch content you’re doing a lot of the same things that you would
do in a sales letter, but you’re spreading it out over time.
!
What you’re doing is you’re building a relationship, you’re delivering real value, you’re
delivering really great content as you move through this.
!
The Sideways Sales Letter allows you to do everything you do down in here, but you
get people to pay attention and you’re building rapport as you go along. It gives you a
chance to hit the mental triggers. We’ll be getting to the triggers a little later in this
module. These mental triggers are the things that cause people to act. They’re things
like authority and reciprocity, and social proof, and scarcity, and community.
!
In these prelaunch videos, they give you the opportunity to deploy those mental
triggers and to build a relationship.
!
One of the things they really do is they really help you with the good old B.S. detector.
Basically, think about when you walk onto a used car lot and the salesman is coming
out to walk towards you. Generally, your B.S. detector is going off the chart, you don’t
necessarily believe him and you’re up in the red zone. You know he’s coming to sell
you, you don’t have a lot of faith that you can trust him, so you’re just not going to
believe anything he says. Everything he says, you’re going to take it with a grain of salt.
When someone comes to your old style sales letter, what happens is... as soon as they
see this page load, as soon as they see this headline and see this huge page load...
their B.S. detector goes into the red zone and they’re not going to believe what you
have to say. They’re going to be very skeptical.
!
What happens when you come out with prelaunch content, and you deliver some really
great value, and then three days later you release another video that teaches, trains,
and gives them real value?

-- 10 of 46 --

!
Then what you do is here we’ve got the green zone. The green zone is like when your
mom is telling you, “Wow, I just ate at a really great restaurant. You should check it
out.” Your mom is telling you that, so you’re probably going to believe her. You don’t
think she has an ulterior motive, you really trust her.
!
Let’s say there’s sort of three zones here. It’s green, black, and red. Here is mom telling
you about a restaurant or whatever, something innocuous. You completely believe over
here. Over here you don’t believe the salesman so much.
!
They come to your long form sales letter, they’re in the red zone immediately. But over
here... what happens is when you start delivering great content here, and giving them
real value in their lives, and you start hitting these mental triggers... then you go from
being in the red zone and you move over. You might not go over into the green zero,
like they’d believe a mom, but they believe you and they trust you a lot more and they
start to like you.
!
The big thing about this sideways sales letter is it basically moves people from
skepticism to trust before you ever get to that sale. It's a sequence of actions.
!
As we go through Product Launch Formula, we’re going to walk through all kinds of
sequences, the timing of this, what to do in each one of these, what to say in each one
of these pieces of prelaunch content.
!
In an Internal Launch, this might be your day one. This is your timeline down here.
Day four. Day seven. And then here you open cart, and this is your sales video or your
sales letter. We might release that on day 10. Again, I’m going to go through all of this
in detail, walk you through all of this as we go through Product Launch Formula.
Different types of launches have different timing, that might just be a typical Internal
Launch.
!
Your first piece of prelaunch content is great for authority. Generally, if you’re giving
out great prelaunch content, you’re teaching and that automatically establishes you as
an authority. It’s just an automatic thing. When you’re publishing, whether it’s
publishing online or publishing in any form, as a publisher people automatically look at
you as an authority. When you can just sit there and give out good content for free, you
are seen as an authority.
!
Another thing, you’ll start to build up reciprocity here. That is if you give someone
something, they will feel some kind of debt to give you something back. Just by giving
them great free content, you’re starting to build this reciprocity. That will come in handy
down the road.
!
I’m going to go through all these triggers and exactly how they work, how you use
them, and what they mean later in this module and as we go through the course. I’m
just being very conceptual here with the Sideways Sales Letter.

-- 11 of 46 --

!
In that first video, you’ll typically also get some social proof. Social proof is this idea if
we see other people doing something, then we think it might be a good thing. Typically
you’ll get comments or you’ll ask for comments on that video or that piece of prelaunch
content. It will be on a blog and people can see those comments. That’s automatic
social proof.
!
This first piece we call the opportunity.
!
That’s where you’re teaching people that there’s an opportunity for change.
!
Real big picture, one of the greatest things I think you can do for someone is show
them that they have an opportunity to change.
!
Show them they can have an opportunity to lower their golf score. Or they have an
opportunity to make more money as a massage therapist. Or they have an opportunity
to have a better relationship or to attract the love of their life. Or the opportunity to
play guitar.
!
You’re showing them that they can change their life. That they can get better at
something, get more of something, reduce their pain.
!
This first piece is generally about the opportunity to get these great results, to get
this transformation.
!
The second one is what we call the transformation video.
!
This is where you start to make it real. Often you’ll have a case study or you’ll do a lot
more heavy duty teaching in the second video. That way you’re basically getting more
authority, reciprocity, and social proof. There’s this idea of likability where people start
to like you more.
!
Often we have a lot of community that starts to build up. The community can actually
build up in the comments. Just people helping each other or answering each other,
building on each other’s comments. You can really start to build some community in
that second piece.
!
This third one is the ownership experience.

What you do is over these seven days, or five days, or 10 days, you really start to build
real trust.

Launch Conversation
!
Our next strategy is the launch conversation.
!
This one might feel a little conceptual, but again we’re going to drill down on this as we
move forward.
!
I told you earlier that list building is the one thing that I stumbled onto that made all
the difference. Well, there’s one other thing. There are two things that have made all
the difference:
! • 	The first was the list building; always focus on the list and list relationship.
• 	The second one is focusing only selling what the market wants.
!
The way you figure out what the market wants is you tap into the conversation.
!

This has actually gotten a lot easier now with social media, because social media is just
one big long conversation. That has made it a lot easier. With launches you can
completely leverage that conversation.
!
If you had to sell something to someone, if your life depended on it, if your family’s
future depended on selling something to someone, the best most effective way to
convert someone to get them to buy is face-to-face.
!
You can sit down with someone and you can talk to them and judge whether they’re
starting to drift away, whether their eyes are glazing over, they’re not paying attention,
you can see what gets them excited and what turns them off.
!
That’s very powerful because now you’re having a conversation. You’re talking to them,
you’re judging their reaction. They can look at you, they can judge you, they can watch
your interest level. Very powerful.
!
The problem is it’s hard to leverage that. If you’re going to sell face-to-face, you can
only do that a few times a day.
!
Then on the other end of the spectrum the way to sell that has the most leverage is
probably TV, where you can just have millions of people see your message all at once.
On the other hand, you don’t have that interaction so you can’t tell what people are
thinking. You get massive leverage, but very little conversion.
!
The product launch with PLF allows a combination of those two. It gives you the big
leverage, maybe not as big as TV, but it gives you a lot of leverage as a one-to-many
type of media. But you’re able to get that conversation by people leaving comments,
the emails, and what they’re sharing in social media. It’s just a beautiful combination of
those two.
That’s where this launch conversation is so strong. It lets you identify what the market
wants through that conversation, through that interaction, through surveys, comments,
social media. And it allows you to identify objections and hot points. It allows you to
identify what their desires are. This makes selling a lot easier.
!
A real key to creating that conversation is to direct it and to ask for it. In every one of
those prelaunch videos at the end of the video you ask for them to make a comment.
Or in between you might be sending out an email, you ask for interaction in the email.
You might have a P.S. like, “P.S. I’d love to know what you’re thinking. Just hit the reply
button right now and let me know.”
!
A lot of times you’ll say, “I’d love to know what you’re thinking,” but people just assume
that it’s a rhetorical statement that you don’t really mean it. But if you say, “P.S. I would
love to know what you think about this. Hit your reply button right now and send me an
email to tell me what you think.” Those are the magic words and you’ll get a lot more

interaction. It’s not like everyone is going to respond to you, or even 50%, but you will

!

!
Launch conversation is really the magic lever that sort of lies inside Product Launch

Robert Collier, a famous copywriter, wrote about this long ago and he said,
!
“You want to enter the conversation in your prospect’s mind.”
!
You want to enter the conversation that’s already going on in their mind. The launch

!
The same thing with your sales video and your emails each step of the way, you’ve

!
The number one core strategy I want to cover here. This would be my number one core
strategy no matter what product, what I was teaching you in terms of business, and
that is the list.
!
Always be focused on your list and building your list.

The reality is the wealth is in the list. For most businesses the wealth is in the list. It’s
the list of prospects and the list of clients or customers.
!
I stumbled onto this early. There were two things I stumbled onto early and this was
one of them. I started literally building my list before I even had a website. That’s how I
stumbled on it early. And I’ve been focused on list building since 1996, so I’ve gotten
pretty good at it.
!
You always want to be building your list, you always want to be focusing on your list,
you always want to be focused on your list building process. You want to develop an
obsession with list building.
!
Now, no one starts out with a list.
!
That’s really important. I wasn’t born with a list. I didn’t come out of the womb with a
list.
!
I have built significant lists in multiple different markets. I’ve built tens of thousands of
person lists in several different markets.
No one starts with a list, but the successful people start building their list early and
often. No one starts with a list, but the smart folks start building that list.
!
One thing to remember is a lot of people obsess that they have to have a big list. They
think, “It’s not going to do any good until I have 1,000 people,” or 10,000 people, or
30,000 people, or 100,000 people. Get that out of your mind. Everyone starts with zero
subscribers and they build it from there.
!
The thing is that small lists are strong. Your results, your conversions from the first
1,000 people are going to be strong. I’ve seen many times in launches that small lists
outperform much bigger lists.
!
It isn’t like a 1,000 person list is going to outperform a 100,000 person list. But a 1,000
person list on a per subscriber basis is generally going to get much better results than
that 100,000 person list. This is just a universal law of the universe.
!
The basics of list building are simple. This is why I include a list building course within
Product Launch Formula, because this is so critical and so important.
!
You need three things:
! 1. 	A squeeze page
2. 	An opt-in bribe - what we call an ethical bribe
3. 	Traffic - you need to put some kind of traffic to that squeeze page.
!

The squeeze page with the ethical bribe is like, “Join my list and I’ll give you this thing,”
so you need to create the page that makes that promise and then you drive traffic to
that page. Boom, when people opt-in to get their bribe, they’re building your list.
!
I’m not going to go into this deep right now because I’m giving you this entire
Launching Your List 2.0 course with Product Launch Formula and we’re going to go
plenty deep there. The thing is always focus on building your list.
!
Launches are a great way for building your list.
I’ve had launches where I have put 30,000 to 50,000 people on my list in a matter of
10 days. That’s awesome and incredible. That’s with a joint venture launch (JV Launch)
where you have lots of other people with big lists mailing into your squeeze page,
mailing into your first piece of prelaunch content, mailing to your second piece of
prelaunch content and third piece of prelaunch content. That’s really awesome.
!
Joint venture launches are great ways to build your list. However, they’re not the only
way launches build your list.
!
We’ve seen this over and over where a well run, well orchestrated, well engineered
internal launch, or even a seed launch, you put out that good content and word starts
to go around and just word of mouth you can build your list.
!
We’ve seen this over and over where people maybe have a 1,000 or 2,000 person list
and they might add 1,000 people onto their list during their launch even without joint
venture partners.
!
This happens more nowadays because of social media. Social media makes it so easy to
share your prelaunch content, so people will share your prelaunch content and the
people they share it with will generally come into your squeeze page and opt-in to get
your prelaunch content. So even outside of joint venture launches, even just internal
launches or seed launches, you can build your list.
Launches are awesome for list building, but remember you should always be
building your list, including when you’re not in a launch.
!
Slow and steady wins the race.
!
When I started publishing about the stock market, initially I got one subscriber every
three or four days. Eventually I worked up and I was getting one subscriber a day, and
that was really exciting. Then I worked and there was sort of a threshold around 3 or 4
subscribers I would average per day. That was pretty cool because that meant I was
getting about 100 people a month who were joining my list and I was really pretty
amped about that.
!

I kept working on it. I worked on bringing in more traffic, I worked on my opt-in

!
I just kept on polishing that thing for years and I eventually built that list up to about

!
I have all kinds of insight from being on the insides of many launches, including big

!

!
When you have the absolute warmest list is after the launch. The day after the launch

Sequences
!
Our next strategy it is sequences.
!
I mentioned this in the sideways sales letter, but that was just one sequence.
!
Sequences are really important. They build anticipation for your product and they pull
people into your marketing. It’s all about turning your marketing into an event. People
love events. If you can get them excited about your event – and generally we can do a
really good job of that with Product Launch Formula – then you win.
!
Again, you don’t want to depend on one interaction. You don’t want to depend on that
Super Bowl ad, you don’t want to depend on that hope marketing. You want to use that
product launch parachute and really crush it with a sequence.
!
There are a number of sequences that we use.First of all is one my favorites, the pre-
prelaunch.
!
Here we use what we call the shot across the bow. You’re going to have to wait until
the next module to learn all about the shot across the bow. The pre-prelaunch is great
because this is where you build early buzz before you even start to get into releasing
prelaunch content.
!
The great thing about this is that your prospects don’t even have any idea that you’re
starting to warm them up for your product with your pre-prelaunch. A lot of times
people try to reverse-engineer my launches and they don’t even notice the pre-
prelaunch.
!
Pre-prelaunch is awesome, it’s usually a very simple short sequence. You use it to find
out what the market wants, to create early buzz, and a lot of times to build up case
studies and to build testimonials.
!
After that comes the prelaunch. That makes sense, right? First you have the pre-
prelaunch and then you have the prelaunch. The prelaunch is that sideways sales letter
I talked about. Generally it’s three pieces of prelaunch content, what we call PLC. These
days a lot of times we use video for the prelaunch content.
!
The whole idea of the prelaunch content is to basically make the sale before you even
open the cart so that when someone actually comes into your sales letter or your sales
video they’re so excited and ready to buy that they don’t even read your sales letter or
look at your video, they just scroll right to the end try to put in their credit card.
!
A lot of times, if you’re doing it right, people in pre-prelaunch will say, “Hey, can I just
give you my credit card and you can just charge it when you’re ready to release this
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 19
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 19 of 46 --

thing?” That’s when you know you’re doing really well, when you get people typing in
their comments and trying to put their credit card into the comments. That’s awesome,
and it actually happens.
!
The next one is the launch. We also call this the open cart.
!
If the prelaunch takes you up into the open cart, then you open the cart, and then
there’s actually a sequence between when you open the cart and when you close the
cart.
!
That sequence between cart opening and cart close, that’s your launch sequence.
!
A lot of times there’s some real blocking and tackling to be done. For everyone
outside the U.S., that’s an analogy to American football.
!
Blocking and tackling, if you look at American football it looks really complicated. I grew
up with it, so I can’t imagine being an adult and trying to watch it for the first time
because it’s complex. At the basic core of it is what they call the blocking and tackling;
knocking over the other guys or having them knock over your guy. That term blocking
and tackling is just a term for let’s get down to the bare fundamentals.
!
A lot of times within the launch it is just blocking and tackling, taking care of business,
and working from the time you open that cart until the time you close cart to keep up
the pressure and keep on building that pressure right until the time when you close
cart.
!
It is important that you do close the cart, this is a sequence. You don’t want to just
open that cart and it stay open forever.
!
**The cart can stay open forever, but the offer, your launch offer has to have
a defined end.**
!
I want to be really clear on this...
!
At the end of your open cart period something “bad” has to happen if the
people don’t buy from you. Either the product goes away and they can’t get it
anymore, or the price goes up, or bonuses go away. There is some defined
end to your open cart period. If you don’t do that, you’re only going to get
half the sales you should have.
!
You will literally double your sales if you have some defined end to your launch. It’s
critically important. Well, it’s only important if you want to get all your sales. If you only
want to get 50% of the sales out there, then don’t worry about closing the cart or
closing the launch offer.
!

Then after your launch you figure you’re done, right?
!
Then there’s what we call the post-launch.
!
This is where you take care of all your new clients. This is where you start to set up
your next launch. By taking care of clients and by taking care of any JV partners, any
content partners during that sequence, and making good on all your promises, that’s
where you set up the next launch. It’s critically important.
!
A lot of times people get down to here and they’re just so excited to be done that they

want to really encourage the conversation with your post-launch period as you’re
delivering the product, because that conversation will give you the ideas for your next
product or your next promotion.
!
You also want to be following up with the new prospects you have, the people that
didn’t buy from you. You’ve been sending them really cool stuff all along, you don’t
want to just go away and leave them alone for months and they’ll be like, “I thought he
loved me, but now I never hear from him anymore.” You don’t want to do that.
!
You want to have a plan for your post-launch to follow up with your new buyers, with
your prospects, and if you had a JV launch also with your JV partners.
!
The next one is the re-launch. Re-launches are just downright awesome, fantastic,
totally groovy, because you usually don’t have to do any more work.
!
You usually can take your prelaunch sequence and maybe slap a new pre-prelaunch on
it, but your prelaunch sequence repeat that maybe three months, six months, a year
down the road. You basically do that same launch and make a whole bunch more
money without a lot of extra effort.
!
These are sort of in order; pre-prelaunch, prelaunch, you just go right down that. This

!
Really what happens in a big JV launch, this sequence actually is all the way up here

Positioning
!
Our next strategy is positioning. That is basically how the market sees you, what they
see as your value or prestige.
!
Think of Chevy versus BMW in the automobile market. Chevy here in the United States
is sort of the every person’s car, just not super budget, but not fancy whatsoever. Then
you have BMW, which they’re not even cars, they’re “the ultimate driving machine,” so
BMW is pretty high end. Then there’s above BMW, you could go to Bentley or Rolls
Royce. These are different layers of positioning.
!
Think about Toyota and Lexus. Same car company really. Toyota wanted to create a
premium brand, they wanted to start creating premium cars, but they already had this
positioning sort of like Chevy with the every person’s car.
!
So rather than trying to recreate their positioning and convince people that now Toyotas
are super high end, they created a new company and called it Lexus. That’s how they
got that positioning, they created this new car and started making really high end cars
called Lexus.
!
It’s a lot like that for anyone in any market and any kind of business. The people in your
market want sort out where are you – are you high end, mid-tier, or low end – where
you fit in.
!
In general, in this market I think you want to be seen as a leader who provides massive
value. That’s the positioning I want and I think that’s the positioning that I have. I think
it’s a great positioning for you to pursue as well. Be seen as a leader, be seen as
someone who over delivers and provides massive value.
!

highest priced, because I want to provide a massive value.

When you do that, high end positioning is a great thing because it allows you to charge

the top of the market. All the best in the market comes to you when you have strong

!
Positioning builds positioning. It’s sort of like being a celebrity in Hollywood. If you

you and you’re going to get paid more for those parts. Then you’re getting these great

!
It’s like deal flow. You’re this actor and you do great in a movie, all of a sudden these

When I think of course correction, I often think of golf.
!
I actually played a bunch of golf when I was a young teen; I worked at a golf course for
a summer and played golf for an entire summer. I’ve hardly played it since then. But it
really is an improbable sport.
!
The thing is you’re sitting there with this little golf ball and you have to hit that ball, on
a Par 5, 500 yards into a hole this big. 500 yards is almost a third of a mile away that
you have to hit this tiny ball into this tiny little hole. It’s just absolutely ludicrous to think
about that. And you’re hitting this with a stick. It’s not easy, it’s just an improbable
sport.
!
The idea of hitting that ball and having it go straight to the hole and taking you four or
five shots and have it go straight every time – first you hit it 250 yards, the next time
you hit it 200 yards, and you hit it straight to the hole – that never happens.
!
Even the greatest golfer in the world on their best hole of their lives, they don’t hit
every shot directly at the hole. What they do is they hit it towards the hole. The
greatest golfers might it directly very close to perfect, but it’s still not perfect. Amateurs,
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 25

they just sort of hit it in that general direction. Then the next time they hit it, they’re
hitting it in the general direction. Each time they’re getting closer to the hole and their
corrections are generally less and less, until they’re standing over a putt. Maybe it’s
taken them 10 or 12 shots to get there, but they’re only a foot away and now it’s a lot
easier to knock it into the hole.
!
You gradually course correct as you get closer. It’s like here’s where you’re starting and
here’s your destination. We all want to do this, but in real life it never happens that way.
In real life, you go like this and you realize that you’re not completely on course, so you
go back here, maybe next time you’re over here, and next time you’re over here, but
you’re gradually getting closer and closer.
!
That’s the funny thing. I’ve been out with a lot of beginning golfers and no matter how
improbable it is, they generally even when they’re first starting out will get the ball in
the hole within 10 or 12 shots. Now, 10 or 12 shots might not be great for an
experienced golfer, but it is sort of improbable that you have this little ball and this little
hole a long ways away and in just 10 or 12 shots by course correcting you gradually get
closer and closer.
!
I’m just trying to encourage you.

Launch Goals
So now I want to talk about your launch goals. As the saying goes, “if you don't know
where you're going, any road will get you there”. Product Launch Formula can serve a
lot of things in your business. It can build your business quickly, but depending on
exactly what you want out of your launch, it will alter your strategy going forward. You
can't create a great strategy until you figure out where you want to go.
This is the first question that I ask my private clients, “what do you want to get out of
your launch?” One of my favorite questions is, “what's a home run for you?”. You have
to know this before you can design your strategy. You can have more than one goal.
You can have a primary goal, and you can also have secondary goals. Those goals will
definitely impact what you do in your launch, i.e. what your launch strategy is.
There's two uber (big goals) - they tend to be the two forks in the road. One of them is
money. A lot of people just assume that a launch is always about money, but it's not
always the case. I have definitely done launches that were about the money. I needed
to make some money, so I performed a launch. As we get deeper in the course I'll
share the Quick Launch, where I got a huge tax bill and I wanted to make the money to
pay it off.
I had another launch where I wanted to finish the basement, so I did a launch to finish
that. I had another launch that, frankly, was going to be the down payment for the
house that I'm living in right now. If it's about the money for you, and certainly for a
long time it was about the money for me, I was just trying to support my family and
create a better lifestyle for them. I wanted to let my wife come home and be with the
kids more. It's fine if it's about the money, and if it is then own it. Say “yeah, I want to
make X number of dollars, or I need to make this money to get this goal.” That is fine.
A lot of launches fit into what I call, the “save the world” category. This is where people
just really want to get their work out into the world. They want to help people, they
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 27
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 27 of 46 --

want to make an impact. Lots and lots of launches fall into this category. If you come to
PLF Live, you think you're going to walk in there and this is all about marketing - and
we give you lots of great marketing - but you're going to meet a lot of people out there
that are doing some amazing world-changing things.
A lot of launches fall into this save the world category. One important thing: saving
the world doesn't preclude making money. It's hard to save the world if you don't
have the money. A lot of times people start off with, “I want to make the money”, and
then they move towards purpose. They want to have a greater purpose. They want to
save the world, but the reality is you need the money both ways. Usually people (even
if they are just about the money) will evolve more towards purpose once they get their
financial and time freedom. Just remember it's a lot easier to save the world if you can
fund your mission.
I just want to be really clear that we've got people going through Project Launch
Formula on both of these categories, and they're both fine. You deciding where you fit
right now for this next launch you're working on will help you nail down your strategy.
Those aren't the only launch goals that are out there. Your launch could be about
building proof or building social proof too.
Years ago, I rolled out this program called Product Launch Manager, and it was about
teaching people how to run launches for other people - how to be product launch
managers. I did that, it was great, we did a great big launch that the world saw, but
first I did an internal launch.
That internal launch wasn't really about making money, it was about building proof and
building social proof. It was about training a small core group of product launch
managers and having them be successful, and having them go through a very high end
exclusive training. Then I could use the proof of their success and the social proof of
how that program sold out at a very high price, and I could use that to roll out my
bigger program.
First I ran the program just for 50 people, and then I rolled it out as a bigger, home
study course for hundreds of people. It was very, very effective. That first launch was
just about building proof and social proof.
Another launch goal might be to attract joint venture partners and affiliates. You build a
great conversion machine with your internal launch, and then you can take those stats,
those metrics and share them with partners, and share them with potential affiliates. It
could be just to attract partners and affiliates.
It could be to create a product. That's really what the Seed Launch is all about -
creating a product. When you first do a Seed Launch, you're not going to make a ton of
money. You can make thousands, you might even make $10,000 or 20,000, but it's not

going to be the launch that sets you up for life. It does allow you to create a

It's also great for building your list. You might do a launch that is specifically just
around building either your client list or your prospect list, or both. We're going to be
getting into all this as we go through the program, but I'm just giving you
different potential goals for your launch.
Another one could be just to gather data. You can gather all kinds of great market data,
figure out what the best squeeze pages and headlines are for your offer. You can use
your comments to help with your offer copy. In fact, no matter what kind of launch you
do, no matter what your launch goals are, you are going to gather all kinds of data, and
you're going to learn an amazing amount that first time you go through a launch. One
of the biggest benefits is that launch conversation. When you see what people are
reacting to, then it helps you plan out your squeeze pages and your offers on your opt
in pages. It helps you get your headline copy right, it helps you figure out what your
offer is.
There's lots of other reasons. Product Launch Formula is great for creating influence,
and influencing other people. No matter what kind of influence you want to create, you
can design a launch to do that for you. Will Hamilton of FuzzyYellowBalls.com, a tennis
instruction site, did this deal with a couple of top-tier pros. These are world famous
professional tennis players. He was going to do a training product with them.
They created the product, then he went to launch it, and he just wanted to make sure
that one launch with those 2 players is a home run. He knew if he did really well for
them they would tell all their other friends (the other professional players), and he
would be able to do that type of product with other players as well.
He designed a launch where he just went all out. In fact, the bonuses he gave were
just over the top. He was willing to just go all out and spend every bit of relationship
capital he had just for that one launch with those 2 players. Just because he knew if he
hit a home run for them, then it would help him get more top players to work with.
That's what he did, he designed a launch that way, and it worked that way, and he got
exactly what he wanted. In fact it led to a deal with a former number one player in the
world creating a product with him.
Another launch - I had a PLF Owner who - he was selling a CRM package (a customer
relationship package) and he was selling it to Fortune 500 companies. These are really
big decisions involving whole committees, and it’s a multiple weeks/months long
decision-making process. No one's going to buy this huge CRM system for a big
company off of a “buy it now” button from a launch video. That's just not the way those
decisions are made.
He used PLF and did a launch for each company, just to get in the door to be able to
make a presentation to them. It was enormously successful. This is an example of a

Asset Identification
!
Now we're going to do some asset identification. This is a fun exercise. You're going to
like this. I think you're going to be pleasantly surprised. As we move towards your
launch, it's important to identify and gather all your assets. That's what we're going to
do here. Most people have assets that they've completely forgotten about, including
me. You already have assets. There are a lot of times people have this 30,000 person
list, for example, that they've completely forgotten about. You almost surely already
have assets.
One of the things that you'll often see in my work is this idea of "dig your well before
you're thirsty." Really what this is about is not only about identifying the assets you
already have, but it's identifying the map of how you can gather more assets. As we go
through here, that's the frame I want you to think about. Not only do I have this, but
what can I do now to start building those assets? Most of the time assets come
from adding value to other people's lives. That's where nearly all of mine have
come from. It's from adding to peoples' lives. A lot of times I get great support when I
go to launch something from joint venture partners and affiliates.
People often wonder how year-after-year I get great support. As we spend a lot of time
helping people out, I see a lot of people that might be starting out coming up in my
industry, a lot of times just out of the blue they all will get an email from me offering to
help them with their launch. You can't hire me, I’m unhirable. People can't pay me after
working their launch, but often I'll just show up and help people out, just because that's
the way I am. Often that comes flowing back to me, even if not from that person; from
some person has heard about me helping someone else. All of a sudden they're willing
to help me. So yes, I've got these amazing assets, but I didn't start with any of them. I
built them up over time. Once you start to focus on this list I'm about to share, then
you're going to have those assets as well.
First of all, I want you think about any potential lists you have; prospect lists, client
lists, offline lists, old lists that you've forgotten about, that you haven't mailed in a long
time. And that if you have that old, cold list, moldy list, that's okay, I'm going to give
you a super quick recipe for warming them in a minute. A lot of time people create a list

that they’ve built over time, and they forget about it. Or you might have some clients

What kind of lists do you have, especially list of people that you've worked with or built

What content relationships do you have? Who do you know that's gotten content

some of their stuff? What about blogs, do you have one? Do you have relationships
with bloggers?
The easiest way to develop a relationship is to leave intelligent comments on a blog,
almost every blogger out there is reading the comments on their blogs. It's almost
magnetic. They can't not read them, they have to read them.
Over time, say over the next month, leave an intelligent comment every time they
publish a new post. We're not being self-promotional, we're not saying, "Oh, I totally
agree with you. It just like I say on my blog here," or "Yeah, I 100% agree with you,
just like I say in my product here, go, here's the link." Don't be promotional, just be
cool, and help their positioning. Help them within your comment. Look at what they're
trying to do with their blog and their post. How are they trying to position themselves?
What are they using their content for? How could you leave an intelligent comment that
helps them accomplish what they want? That’s the big thing with all these assets. The
entire asset identification exercise is all about figuring out how you can help
people get what they want.
When you work on helping them get what they want, all of a sudden you start to
accumulate assets. It might not come pouring in overnight, but that’s how I built my
assets. I'm always focusing on how can I help people get what they're after. Another
way you can build relationships and assets is by doing a guest post for other blogs.
After you built some relationship with them, you left some great comments; maybe you
can contact them and see if you can write a guest post. That can be a great way to
build up your asset.
Another way is forum presence. Forums are old school now, because of social
media, but there are still some very active forums in a lot of markets. For instance, if
you're interested in electric guitars there is this fantastic forum just for people that are
really into Fender Telecasters. There's nowhere in social media that has this many
people that are into Fender Telecasters as there are on that forum. It's easy to find high
value forums. Just go to Google and search whatever your keyword is, and then the
word “Forum.” You're going to find the forums.
When you get into forums, there's always a group norms. A good forum is always a
strong community, and there are group norms. Get on there, and just read for a few
days or weeks. Observe what the group norms are. A lot of time there are sticky notes
posted in forums where they have what the community norms and rules are. Be sure to
check those out. Remember one thing - add value. Don't promote until you've added
value in the forum. Do you have a presence on a forum or could you build a presence?
What kind of testimonials and case studies do you have? They can be for you,
your product, or your service. Maybe you’ve been working with clients one-on-one.
Now, you want to release a product, but you don't have testimonials for it because it's
not done yet.

You can go back to your clients and get them to say cool things about you and your

Joint venture-affiliates relationships - we’ll go way deeper on this in a later

one way to get on someone’s radar if you want them to promote for you. The second
way is to find a way to build value in their lives.
This could mean buying their product, doing well with it and then being a case study for

A lot of the time people come to me and say, "Jeff, how do I get a whole bunch of

corporation or some store and said, "Hey, I want you to be my champion. Can you be
my champion? Can you be my partner? Can you be my affiliate? Can you promote for
me?"
They'll be like, "Well, who are you?" In normal business, especially offline business, you

different than any other business. Start thinking - are there people that you have added

Or, do you know people who have connections to those people? That can be
the quick route right up to the top in your market. Do you have relationships, or can

It's all about adding value. Do you have expertise in an area that you can leverage? For

Maybe it was just a quick hallway conversation where I give them an idea at a

get great sponsors for his launch and create great prices for his Joint Venture partners.
He went from not having much positioning, to being able to offer these amazing prices
for Joint Venture partners who took part in his launch. This put him on the map right
away.

Do you have relationships with experts? Often there are people who are experts
in an area, they might not be a huge key player or person of influence, but they have
great expertise. They're often easy to get interviews with them. Often this is the case
with bestselling authors. There are lots of authors out there whose books aren't selling
a million copies. Even if they're a bestseller in Amazon, they want a bigger audience. If
you go to them, interview them, and promise that you could bring them to a bigger
audience, then it can be a great asset to build up.
John Gallagher is a very famous PLF owner that went from food stamps to building up a
serious business. He initially built up his business by going to experts in his market,
which was edible and medicinal plants and herbs. He went to them and interviewed
them. When he told his small email list about these interviews, they told all their friends
and neighbors because the interviews were with top-tier experts. Experts who didn't
really have any platform or leverage, but they were top tier experts. He was able to
interview them and it didn't cost him anything. They loved doing the interviews because
it brought them to a wider audience. He was able to quickly grow his list by doing that,
and there are many ways to leverage these interviews.
Another asset is Product Launch Formula, the PLF community, and PLF Live.
If you just go to the community and you're looking for experts, or looking to bring your
expertise to people and you mention that in the community, you can often build up
great relationships there. You may find very high power relationships, possibly even
Joint Venture relationships. When you come to PLF Live, which is once a year live event,
the level of Joint Venture deals, content relationships, and ability to get the attention of
people with amazing platforms is just through the roof. This is an absolutely
extraordinary asset.
Right here within Product Launch Formula community you have a great asset. Again,
like everything else it is all about adding value. How do you add value to people's lives?
It's not just about going in there and putting up a post in the PLF community saying,
"Hey I've got this product. Who wants to mail it for me? Who wants to promote for me?
Who wants to be my partner? Who wants to be my affiliate? Who can promote me?”
It's about going in there and adding value - becoming a presence there over time and
then sharing.
In other words, sharing your value and then looking to support other people. Then, and
only then, asking for their support. Obviously it's a common theme here. It's all about
building value for others. If you don't already have these assets in place it's about
building value first. Here is the quick start. Start on all the long-term stuff immediately;
then start publishing on social media. Start building up your presence in forums and in
your niche. Identify those top blogs. Start making intelligent comment. There's a saying
about when is the best time to start…If you want to have a 100 feet oak tree in your
yard, when is the best time to plant it? 50 years ago. That was the best time to plant it.
50 years ago.

-- 34 of 46 --

When is the second best time? Today, you want to start planting today. If you haven't
built up all these assets over time, then do the second best thing. Start building today.
Identify ten experts in your market. Rank them in order of preference with the most
powerful or the best fit at number one. Often the most powerful one might not be a
good fit for you. You might not resonate with their values or their presence and the kind
of content they publish. In that case then, don't go to them.
Then you want to list them in terms of, “these are the best people, the most powerful
people who are a great fit with me”. List them 1-10 in order of power, order of
preference, order of how they resonate with you. You don't go to the number one first.
Approach number ten and ask for an interview. Often they'll do that interview for free.
Or if they don't want to, you could even offer to pay them.
Often it's amazing how small an amount of money you can pay them. After you get
number 10, then go to number 9, and then go to number 8. You can this concurrently.
You can go to number 8 and 9 at the same time. Get that first one done. Just go
through the process. Figure it out. Smooth out all the wrinkles. Get that first one done.

That's a quick look at your asset identification, and a quick start on your asset building.

Warming Your Cold List
Now we’re going to talk about warming a cold list, and this is amazingly easy. A cold list
is basically a list that you’ve built up, but then you stopped mailing it so it gets cold.
People may think, “Who are you?”. They forget about you very quickly. Almost everyone
has let a list go cold. The reality is that once you start a list, you’ll usually end up with
more than one list. I could go into the reasons, but just trust me on it. You usually end
up with more than one list. Almost everyone who starts building a list ends up with
more than one list, and we’ve almost all let a list go cold by not publishing to it. It’s the
reality. I know it sounds weird.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 35
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 35 of 46 --

If you’re sitting there and you don’t have a list (all you want is to have a list of X
number of subscribers) to let it go cold seems like really weird, but trust me it happens.
The thing to remember is for every week or month that a list goes unmailed, it gets
colder. If you let a week go by, it starts to cool off. If you let two weeks go by, it’s
getting colder. You let a month go by, it’s getting colder. That’s okay because there is an
easy solution to that.
This is how you warm a cold list. The “mea culpa”, which means basically
that’s where you just admit your mistake. What you do is you admit your mistake,
you reintroduce yourself, and you tell them why you haven’t contacted them. It doesn’t
have to be a great reason. It could just be, “Hey, it’s Jeff. You subscribed to my updates
a few months ago, and I screwed up. I haven’t sent you anything in months, and I’m
really sorry about that, but my dog got sick, and I got real busy,” and yada, yada.
You just give them the reason. Doesn’t have to be a great reason, just has to be a
reason why. It could just be I just got really busy, but you give them some reason.
Then some revelation of self means telling them a little bit about you and who you are,
your situation, my dog got sick, this happened, that happened might not be a bad thing
here. Then you send them two or three pieces of really cool content.
Frankly, what I would do is send the first email and tell them “Hey, this is who I am,
this is why I haven’t mailed you, but to make it up, I’ve got this really cool thing for
you”. Right in that very first piece, I would give them a cool piece of content. Then send
them another piece of really cool content, maybe two more pieces of cool content. Then
you can ask them to opt in for some more extra cool content.
None of this is rocket science. You could just send them one email that introduced
yourself, gave the reason why, then gave them some really good content, and then
asked them to opt in for some more cool bonus content. Basically with that old list your
open rates and your click rates are not going to be great. You’re just trying to migrate
some of those people over to your new list. Then your new list will be warmer, the open
rates will be better, the click rates will be better, and that will actually make your
delivery rates better.
I know this could be getting a little complicated for a few folks, but just in general, you
want to move people to a new list. Give them some good content and then promise
them some even more cool content. It doesn’t have to be something amazing. It’s not
like you’re giving them some amazing course. It could just be a quick video. It could be
a quick PDF download or something like that.
Set up a new list and have them opt into that list. Then keep in mind that your new list
and old list are very, very different now. Your old list is an old cold list. The new list is
the people that have migrated over and stuck with you. Best case scenario, take the
people that have moved to your new list off your old list. Now they’re just on your new
list, unsubscribe them from the old list. Then every now and then, you can send your
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page
!
36
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 36 of 46 --

old list another email telling them about some more cool content and try to get them to
move over.
With your first email to your old list, there aren’t going to be that many people opening
or clicking, but you’ll still move some people over. Then a week later, you can mail them
again and you’ll get some more people to move over. A week after that, same thing. If
you’ve had a list that you let go cold for six months or longer, don’t think you’re going
to get the majority of those people moving over. You’re going to get a very small
percentage of them, but it is going to be a very engaged, very strong percentage. This
is worth doing, and it’s really that simple. That’s how you warm a cold list.

Your Avatar, Part 1
We’re now going to talk about your Avatar, and this is some of the most important,
most powerful work you’ll do within this module and probably within the entire Product
Launch Formula.
Your avatar, is a composite representation of your ideal client.
Who is your average client? Sometimes you might have more than one avatar. For
example, in Product Launch Formula I have more than one avatar. I have people that
already have an existing online business and they want to leverage it. They want to get
better results.
I also have people that want to start an online business and are just starting out. Those
are two very different avatars. I've got more avatars - artists, authors, practitioners,
people with offline businesses that want to move online. So as you can see I have
several different avatars.
If you have more than one avatar (and you probably do) you might want to do this
exercise we're about to go through for each avatar. This is really powerful stuff. This
concept of being really clear on who you're trying to reach is super important.
It can be really helpful to think of your avatar as a real person. I know some
people recommend giving them a real name, and talk about them as if they're a specific
person. That can be really powerful because in your marketing you always want to be
talking to one person.
You don't want to be writing to your list or recording videos thinking you’re writing an
email to 6,000 people. No, you're writing that email for 6,000 individual people who are
going to go check their email, real individual people who are going to open up their
email program and see your email in their inbox. This is a very private place for them,
and they're going to read it to see if you connect with them.

!
Now I want to talk about the circle of awesome, because it’s just plain awesome.
!
This is the deal. I don’t want you thinking about just one launch.
!
Yes, we have to get that first launch under your belt. But I want you to think in terms of

!
This is how John Gallagher goes from food stamps to having a million dollar business.

There’s this thing called a JV launch down here. JV launches you can make a lot of
money because you’re tapping into other people’s lists. JV means joint venture. You can
use affiliates or joint venture partners. Then they mail their lists about your launch.
!
What if you had a 10,000 person list, but you could find five joint venture partners and
each of those people had a 20,000 person list, and all of a sudden 100,000 people
between the five of them are being mailed to instead of just the 10,000 people on your
list. It gives you a universe that’s literally 10 times bigger than your universe with just
five partners. Something like that is not unusual.

The biggest launch I ever did before my first JV launch was an internal launch of
$110,000. My first JV launch was over $600,000, so it was a factor of 6X. My next joint
venture launch after that was almost $4,000,000, so that’s a factor of 40X over my
internal launches. So joint venture launches can be really great.
!
The thing is you don’t want your first launch to be a joint venture launch, because you
want to test your launch, you want to test your prelaunch content, you want to test
your sideways sales letter to make sure it converts before you ask partners to mail for
you. If those partners mail for you, you want to make a lot of money for them so they
will want to mail for you in the future.
!
That means that you don’t test out your launch on a JV launch.
!
First, you do an internal launch.
!
You do an internal launch and you figure out your offer, you figure out your
prelaunch content, you figure out the whole sequence, you figure out your
open cart sequence, you figure out how to take care of people after they buy
from you.
!
With the circle of awesome what you do is you start off with the internal launch and
you test the process.
!
Maybe you have a list of 1,000 people. Let’s just say you have 1,000 people on your list.
You do this internal launch and you do, let’s say, $20,000 on that internal launch.
!
Then what you do is you go to your joint venture partners and you say, “Hey, I’ve got
this product. I only have 1,000 people on my list, but I was able to sell $20,000. That’s
$20 per name on my list. I can’t tell for sure whether these numbers will hold up, but if
you mail people into my launch then I’m paying you a 50% commission, if the numbers
hold up that means for every person that joins my list during this launch you’re going to
make $10.”
!
When you go to a partner with those kind of numbers and those kind of metrics, then it
makes it a lot easier to track the partners. Which $20,000 is awesome for most people.
You do that and then you go to the JV launch.
!
Now say you have five partners and each of those five partners has 20,000 people on
their list, so 100,000 people get mailed. Of those 100,000 people – I’m just making

to 6,000, you added 5,000 people onto your list. Now with 6,000 if those numbers held
up you might do $100,000 on this launch.
!
You just created this product once and you were able to do this $20,000 and then

first time I rolled PLF out to the public. I built a list of about 15,000 people, and then I
just went from there.
Another way you could do it is you could do the seed launch over here. Do your initial
seed launch, build up that initial seed product, get that initial tiny little list that’s built
through that seed launch process, and then go to an internal launch from there and
then take it to a JV launch.
!
There’s a lot of different ways, but these three can tie together. That’s why in this
course I’m teaching that seed launch first, then we’re going to go through the internal
launch and the JV launch, so you can totally get this circle of awesome and put it to use
in your business, because it is 100% awesome.
!
It’s this vortex of goodness, of momentum that builds in your business.
!
There you go, the circle of awesome.
!
